_comment: Curated housing production bill list. Editorial decisions documented inline. Add bills after review. Remove session entries when superseded by new data.

bills.description: H4707 final passage — Affordable Homes Act, 145-13
bills.rationale: Most important single vote. Final passage of the Affordable Homes Act.

bills.description: H4977 conference report — enacted version of Affordable Homes Act, 128-24
bills.rationale: The actual law. Conference committee report accepted before sending to governor.

bills.description: Amendment 29 — Lombardo: exempt 40B-compliant towns from MBTA Communities Act, defeated 27-131
bills.rationale: Anti-housing weakening attempt targeting MBTA Communities enforcement. NAY = pro-housing.

bills.description: Amendment 129 — Jones (Minority Leader): make MBTA Communities evasion easier, defeated 32-126
bills.rationale: Anti-housing weakening attempt from Minority Leader. NAY = pro-housing.

bills.description: Consolidated A — preserve ADU by-right language, passed 130-28
bills.rationale: ADU by-right is core housing production. YEA = pro-housing.

bills.description: Amendment 348 — Frost: count mobile homes toward 40B threshold, defeated 30-127
bills.rationale: NIMBY tactic to inflate 40B numbers and resist new development. NAY = pro-housing.

bills.description: YIMBY Act (193rd) — An Act to promote Yes in My Backyard, 33 cosponsors
bills.rationale: Proactive co-sponsorship of comprehensive zoning reform bill. 33 House cosponsors.

bills.description: YIMBY Act (194th) — current session, 20 cosponsors as of March 2026
bills.rationale: Live political signal. Co-sponsoring the active YIMBY bill right now.
